exemplar.title:
What is the impact of the internal air pressure and the material properties of
a basketball on its rebound height?

detailedExemplarData.title:
What is the impact of the internal air pressure and the material properties of
a basketball on its rebound height?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is stated clearly with independent and dependent variables defined.
Background and introduction maintain a tight focus on the RQ throughout.
Methodology is detailed with justified pressure range, apparatus list, and uncertainty treatment.
weaknesses: Procedural choices (manual drop, spin mitigation) are not optimally justified or controlled.
Pressure gauge accuracy and camera technical specs are not fully integrated into uncertainty analysis.
Methodology, while competent, lacks expertâlevel optimisation (e.g. fixedârelease mechanism).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic clarity and RQ) clearly met topâband descriptors; the methodology strand is strong but not fully optimised. Balancing two band-6 assessments against one band-5 yields a holistic score of 5/6.

strengths: Consistent and coherent application of key physics concepts (energy conservation, restitution, ideal gas law).
Terminology and symbols are defined, used accurately and consistently across the report.
Integration of syllabus theory with experimental data enhances conceptual clarity.
weaknesses: Mislabeling of an inverseâexponential trend as logarithmic.
Omission of units for constants A and B in linear fits.
Minor algebraic sign inconsistency in derivations.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both subjectâknowledge and terminology strands are solidly within the 5â6 band, with only minor inaccuracies preventing a top-band mark, yielding 5/6.

strengths: Data are processed thoroughly with propagated uncertainties and compared to theoretical models.
Arguments link theory, design, and results in a coherent narrative.
Integration of ratio analyses effectively validates hypotheses about material properties.
weaknesses: Statistical analysis is basic (no RÂ² values or confidence intervals explicitly reported).
Some interpretations (e.g. curve shape) are imprecise or mislabeled.
Critical evaluation is largely descriptive and confined to end sections rather than woven throughout.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research analysis and argument development are strong (approaching band-7/8), but evaluation remains limited (band-4â6). A mid-band holistic judgment of 6/12 reflects these mixed performances.

strengths: Report structure follows standard conventions (title page, contents, numbered sections).
Figures and tables are well labeled, captioned, and integrated into the text.
Layout and formatting generally support clear communication.
weaknesses: Table of contents lacks accurate page numbers.
Typographical errors and misaligned tables disrupt professionalism.
Some graphs omit error bars and RÂ² values, hindering quantitative interpretation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While overall presentation meets many top-band descriptors, recurring minor formatting issues and omissions warrant a score one band below maximum, at 3/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Technical terminologyââcoefficient of restitution,â âimpulse,â âtrimmed-sphere approximation,â âlogarithmic fit,â etc.âis used fluently and correctly. Symbols are defined and units handled properly. Occasional typographical slips (âintruigedâ) or informal phrasing do not impede scientific precision. Consistency across 40-page document shows clear command of concepts, aligning with the descriptors for accurate and consistent terminology.

The argument develops logically from theory to design, to results, to conclusion. Figures and tables are integrated and referenced; competing explanations (energy losses, material elasticity) are weighed.  The narrative remains coherent but could be more concise, and some claims (82 kPa âoptimalâ) are qualified yet still rest on limited surface conditions.  Overall structure and reasoning are strong but not at the most sophisticated level, hence an 8.

The methodology exhibits breadth and relevance: full variable table, detailed apparatus list, clear step-by-step procedure, justification of pressure range, and repeated trials for two ball types. Appropriate data-processing routines (error propagation, graphing, logarithmic/regression fits) are shown.  Limitations are acknowledged, yet certain choices (manual drop, iPhone frame rate, uncontrolled spin despite mitigation) reveal that the design, while competent, is not fully optimised or justified at an expert level.  Consequently the strand fits the upper portion of the 3-4/5-6 continuum; a score of 5 reflects a complete but not exemplary methodology.

The essay applies core physics conceptsâenergy conservation, momentum, Hookeâs law, ideal gas law, coefficient of restitutionâand links them coherently to the basketball context.  Derivations are mostly correct and integrated with experimental outcomes.  Minor simplifications (ideal gas, ignoring rotation) are noted but not quantitatively addressed, preventing a flawless level of understanding.  Overall, knowledge application is consistently relevant and largely accurate, justifying a solid 5 within the 5-6 band.

Evaluation addresses sources of error (camera FPS, spin, ideal gas assumption) and suggests improvements (high-speed camera, court surfaces).  However, critique is confined mainly to end sections and is more descriptive than deeply analytical; quantitative impact of each limitation is not estimated.  Continuous critical evaluation throughout the essay is limited, so the strand sits comfortably in the 4-6 band at a score of 5.

The student states the investigation focus (âMaterial and Air Pressureâ) prominently and repeats a concise, unambiguous research question in both the title block and introduction. The background clearly frames the sporting context, defines key variables (rebound height, pressure) and introduces relevant physics principles, giving the reader a coherent picture of purpose and scope. Throughout the essay the student maintains this focus, consistently returning to the RQ in theory, procedure and conclusion. Hence the work meets the descriptor of being âcommunicated accurately and effectively with clear purpose and focus.â

Visual elementsâincluding schematic diagram, experimental photos, multiple plotted graphsâare legible, captioned and referenced in-text. Tables are aligned and units/uncertainties stated.  The layout supports reader understanding, fulfilling criteria for the highest band despite minor resolution issues in a few embedded images.

The research questionââWhat is the impact of the internal air pressure and the material properties of a basketball on its rebound height?ââis sharply defined, contains the independent and dependent variables, and explicitly indicates the comparative element (two materials).  The subsequent discussion, modelâbuilding and analysis all revolve tightly around this RQ, demonstrating a strong and sustained linkage.  Any reader can immediately understand what is being tested and why, so the strand merits the top band.

Data are processed into multiple calculated variables (âh, e, E) with propagated uncertainties, then plotted and compared with theoretical expectations and literature.  The analysis explains observed trends, links graphs to model predictions and discusses systematic deviations (negative intercept).  While largely correct, some interpretations (calling inverse-exponential curve âlogarithmicâ) are imprecise and the statistical treatment stays basic (no explicit RÂ², confidence intervals).  Thus the work demonstrates âgood research analysis clearly relevantâ edging toward excellent, meriting a mid-band 8.

The report follows EE conventions: title page with word count, table of contents, numbered sections, labelled tables/figures, bibliography and appendix.  Equations are formatted, and headings are hierarchical.  Minor formatting inconsistencies (occasional overlap in table borders, long paragraphs) exist but do not hinder clarity.  Consequently the work clearly meets the top presentation band.

annotations.comment: The research question is stated clearly and precisely, highlighting both independent and dependent variables and providing a focused inquiry into rebound height.
The student adheres to the 4000-word limit with a clear word count provided, demonstrating compliance with formatting requirements.
The table of contents lacks page numbers for sections, hindering quick navigation. Consider adding accurate page references.
The background information effectively situates the investigation within FIBA standards and the IB syllabus, demonstrating strong subject knowledge.
Figure 1 lacks annotated axes and clear labels for energy stages. Add descriptive labels to improve interpretability.
The derivation $$mg\Delta h = \tfrac12 mv^2$$ omits discussion of rotational kinetic energy losses; include this to strengthen the energy analysis.
The citation for âThe Interestingâ is incomplete. Provide full reference details in the bibliography for clarity.
The conservation of momentum derivation uses many inline symbols; consider a concise table of symbols and units for improved readability.
The algebra in the derivation of $$e$$ shows a sign inconsistency between stages. Re-derive carefully to confirm the correct expression.
The trimmedâsphere approximation is introduced qualitatively; include a mathematical relation for deformation distance \(x\) versus pressure.
Typographical error in âintriquedâ undermines professionalism; correct to âintriguedâ and proofread for consistency.
Constants \(A\) and \(B\) in \(E = AP+B\) appear without unit clarification. Specify units to ensure dimensional consistency.
The hypothesis about material elasticity lacks quantitative material property values (e.g., bladder stiffness). Include data to support your claim.
Assuming negligible spin should be backed by a measurement method. Propose a quantitative spinâcontrol technique.
Include the pressure gaugeâs accuracy (Â±5 kPa) when listing the independent variable to fully justify the chosen range.
The controlledâvariables table is dense and misaligned; consider splitting into smaller tables or summarizing key points to improve readability.
Provide technical camera details (sensor size, lens focal length) to justify video analysis accuracy.
Switching to a fixed-release mechanism for dropping the ball would reduce height uncertainty compared to manual drops.
Raw data table does not explain how uncertainty was calculated. Clarify the range/2 method and justify its use.
Define a numerical criterion for outlier removal rather than a descriptive approach to ensure reproducibility.
Uncertainties in computed \(E\) grow significantly at high pressures; discuss how this impacts the reliability of the linear fit.
The step-by-step processing for the indoor basketball is thorough and enhances transparency in data handling.
Referencing uncertainty calculations only in the appendix disrupts flow; summarize key formulas here as well.
Graph 2 omits error bars and a reported RÂ² value; include these to quantify the goodness of fit.
The discussion mislabels an inverse-exponential trend as logarithmic. Clarify the correct functional form and terminology.
Calculate the theoretical intercept \(B\) from the model and compare it with the experimental negative y-intercept to deepen critical analysis.
The ratio analysis elegantly highlights material differences, effectively validating the hypothesis about outdoor vs. indoor ball elasticity.
Integration of ratio data with graph 4 provides clear validation of the section 2.3 hypothesis on material properties.
Discuss explicitly how applying the calculated optimal \(e\) to real court conditions (surface, temperature) may differ from your experimental setup.
The reference list shows inconsistent citation styles and abbreviation formats. Adopt a single citation style (e.g., APA) for uniformity.
